scope: LFA-P3 first strict large-file reduction run

reviewer.mode: bounded read-only sharded review plus focused delta review

blocking_findings.summary: File-level fixture state whitelist could silently allow future createWorkbenchServer fixture calls inside helper or shard-10.
Reviewer asked to verify whether currentSessionWorkflowState returned a fresh object before accepting helper mutation.

fixes_after_initial_review.summary: State-boundary now requires every allowFixtureFileState: true call in an allowed file to have a nearby workbench-state-boundary-allow fixture-file-state comment.
State-boundary tests now cover imports without calls, unapproved fixture callers, allowed files without annotations, tool callers, and explicit SQLite-backed callers.
currentSessionWorkflowState reads and JSON.parse's the fixture file on each call, so helper mutations operate on a fresh object.

non_blocking_findings.summary: The annotation checker scans only the preceding three lines; all real usages are immediately adjacent.
The annotation check is a substring match; deliberate malicious embedding could bypass the intent, but the current project threat model treats this as non-blocking.
